prompt: I'm writing a Submit button handler for a long-running planogram copy operation in my OA control. The operation can take a while so I want to use a job queue. How do I handle the difference between a SaaS (BlueYonder-hosted) environment and an on-prem installation?

assertions.name: reads-deploymentmode-from-config
assertions.description: Reads DeploymentMode (or equivalent) from config rather than hardcoding the mode — e.g. IsSaaS() helper or ConfigurationHelper.GetConfigSetting("DeploymentMode")

assertions.name: always-inserts-job-first
assertions.description: Job row is always inserted into the job table (cx_job or similar) regardless of SaaS vs OnPrem — both paths insert first for tracking/auditing

assertions.name: saas-calls-process-proc-immediately
assertions.description: On the SaaS branch, the process proc is called immediately in the same web request (synchronous path) — not deferred to a service

assertions.name: onprem-defers-to-service
assertions.description: On the OnPrem branch, only the insert happens — processing is deferred to a background service/Automator that polls the job table
